strategy_notes: DERISKING PHASE — Rally failed Day 3 follow-through test. Key principles: (1) ALL MACD signals remain bearish — this is the strongest signal in the current setup. No new buys until at least 2-3 names show MACD bullish crossovers. (2) Sold AMZN to raise cash to ~41% — appropriate for an unconfirmed, fading rally. (3) Remaining 3 positions (NVDA, GOOGL, META) are the highest-conviction mega-cap tech names. (4) Profit-taking rule: take 50% off any position at +5% from entry. (5) Hard stops: 6% from entry on all positions. (6) Next add criteria: only buy if (a) MACD crosses bullish on target name AND (b) SPY shows 2+ consecutive up days with volume. (7) If SPY closes below $640 (roughly -2.4% from here), trim all positions by 50%. (8) NVDA is the strongest — last to trim. META is next trim candidate if weakness continues. (9) Cash is a position — don't feel pressure to be fully invested in unconfirmed rallies.

recent_trades.reason: Adding to NVDA position using pyramiding approach. NVDA +5.59% today, leading the semiconductor rally. AI/datacenter secular tailwind is intact. If the Iran de-escalation narrative holds, growth/cyclical tech will continue leading. This is a confirmation add — the initial entry was yesterday and it's already confirming with strong momentum. Scaling up per pyramiding research: add to winners showing confirmation.

Adding to META position. +6.67% today — strongest performer in the top 50. Mega-cap tech is leading this rotation with strong ad revenue fundamentals and AI investments. Pyramiding into a winner that's confirming. If this rally has legs, META will be among the biggest beneficiaries of a sentiment shift.

GOOGL +5.14% today — strong participation in the tech rally. Diversifying cyclical exposure across mega-cap tech rather than concentrating in just two names. GOOGL benefits from same AI/advertising tailwinds as META but adds search/cloud diversification. Initial position size per pyramiding strategy — start moderate, add on confirmation next cycle.

ABBV -0.94% today on a +1.06% SPY day — significant relative weakness. Defensive healthcare is clearly rotating out as the market shifts to cyclical/growth leadership. Still +2.41% from entry, so locking in a small win. This frees ~$12.5K for better-positioned names. Following the proven pattern: sell defensives showing negative relative strength on rally days (same signal that correctly flagged COST and XOM exits).

AMZN +2.11% today — participating strongly in the cyclical rally. Diversifies tech exposure beyond pure advertising (META/GOOGL) into e-commerce/cloud (AWS). AMZN benefits from consumer spending recovery if geopolitical resolution boosts confidence. Starting at 18% per pyramiding approach — moderate initial size, will add on confirmation next cycle. This fills the 5th position slot freed by ABBV sale.

AMZN is the weakest position at -1.38% P&L, down -0.38% today on a flat market day. Strategy notes explicitly flagged AMZN as 'first trim candidate if portfolio needs derisking.' Day 3 of the rally is showing no follow-through, and AMZN is underperforming all other holdings. Cutting the weakest name frees ~$18K to cash, reducing portfolio exposure from ~77% to ~59%. In MACD-unconfirmed environments, protecting capital is priority #1. Taking a small loss (-1.38%) is better than waiting for the -6% stop.

last_decision.market_assessment: SPY and QQQ are essentially flat (+0.09% and +0.11%), suggesting the post-correction rally is losing momentum on Day 3. Every single stock in the universe shows MACD bearish signals (↓), confirming the rally remains technically unconfirmed. This is the critical Day 3-4 follow-through window I identified last cycle — and the market is NOT following through with conviction. The divergence between leaders (INTC +4.89%, AMD +3.47%, NFLX +3.25%) and laggards (TSLA -5.42%, GE -3.94%, ABBV -2.86%) suggests rotation and indecision rather than broad-based buying.
last_decision.self_reflection: My current positions are mixed: NVDA +1.29% and GOOGL +1.29% are modestly green, while AMZN -1.38% and META -0.65% are pulling back. The strategy notes from last cycle explicitly said 'Day 3-4 must show follow-through or this is a dead cat bounce' — and we're seeing flat/mixed action, not follow-through. AMZN was flagged as the weakest position and it's confirming that with -0.38% today while SPY was flat. I was right to be cautious about the MACD-unconfirmed rally, and my research showed fixed percentage targets (5-7%) outperform trailing stops in this setup. None of my positions have hit those targets yet, so the question is whether to hold for potential confirmation or start trimming the weakest names. My 75% win rate is strong but built on disciplined exits — I should honor that discipline now.

decisions.reason: AMZN is the weakest position at -1.38% P&L, down -0.38% today on a flat market day. Strategy notes explicitly flagged AMZN as 'first trim candidate if portfolio needs derisking.' Day 3 of the rally is showing no follow-through, and AMZN is underperforming all other holdings. Cutting the weakest name frees ~$18K to cash, reducing portfolio exposure from ~77% to ~59%. In MACD-unconfirmed environments, protecting capital is priority #1. Taking a small loss (-1.38%) is better than waiting for the -6% stop.

NVDA is the strongest position at +1.29% with solid +0.93% today. Semiconductor sector showing strength (AMD +3.47%, INTC +4.89%). AI/datacenter thesis intact. Will hold with 6% hard stop from entry (~$164.62). If NVDA breaks above $180, that would be a strong confirmation signal.

GOOGL +1.29% from entry but -0.54% today — mild weakness. Analyst consensus is Strong Buy with $351 target (18.8% upside). Holding for now but watching closely. If GOOGL drops below $285 (roughly -2.4% from entry), will exit. The -0.54% today on a flat market is concerning but not yet actionable.

META -0.65% from entry, down -0.82% today. Strong Q4 earnings (revenue +23.8% YoY) support fundamentals. However, it's the second weakest position after AMZN. Holding with tight 5% stop from entry ($549.32). If Day 4-5 doesn't show bounce, META becomes next trim candidate. Research showed first-week leaders don't always sustain — monitoring closely.

last_decision.next_research: Sector rotation timing signals — specifically how to identify when mega-cap tech leadership is exhausting and when to rotate into value/cyclical names, using breadth indicators, relative strength divergences, and volume analysis during the transition from rally to consolidation phases
